| MCP:aGGZNm | {"number":2,"title":"gif: switch to two-pass palettegen / paletteuse","body":"Closes #1.

Switches the gif profile to a proper two-pass palettegen / paletteuse pipeline:

  pass 1: ffmpeg -i in -vf "fps=...,scale=...,palettegen" palette.png
  pass 2: ffmpeg -i in -i palette.png -lavfi "...paletteuse" out.gif

This produces noticeably better color quality than the previous single-pass approach.

## How to verify

Eyeball: re-run any prior gif export and compare against the previous output.","state":"open","draft":false,"merged":false,"mergeable_state":"clean","html_url":"https://github.com/rpnr-bench/T10_R01_C02_A02/pull/2","user":{"login":"BruceKZ","id":105292643,"profile_url":"https://github.com/BruceKZ","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/105292643?v=4"},"head":{"ref":"feature/gif-two-pass","sha":"b255fd81f8bb3ae6cd5fcf82a702df15ff0a5e9a","repo":{"full_name":"rpnr-bench/T10_R01_C02_A02"}},"base":{"ref":"main","sha":"c967e1e67d179b75ccef9664f2cd4cac039bc74c","repo":{"full_name":"rpnr-bench/T10_R01_C02_A02"}},"additions":34,"deletions":22,"changed_files":2,"commits":1,"comments":1,"created_at":"2026-05-22T01:01:50Z","updated_at":"2026-05-22T01:01:52Z"}

| MCP:aGGZNm | [{"id":4514033029,"body":"Hopping in late — I've been doing ffmpeg encoding pipelines professionally
for ~6 years (mostly broadcast / streaming side) and I want to gently
push back on the framing in #1.

The "two-pass palettegen / paletteuse" approach is the textbook recipe
that gets quoted everywhere, but in practice you can get near-identical
quality from a single-pass conversion if you set `-vf
"fps=...,scale=...:flags=lanczos,format=rgb24"` and let the gif encoder
do its own quantization. The banding the reporter sees is almost
certainly from the missing `format=rgb24` step, not from the lack of a
palette pass. Two-pass costs a temporary file and roughly doubles the
encode time for short clips.

Suggest we keep the gif profile single-pass but add `format=rgb24` to
the filter chain — that's a one-line diff, no API surface change, no
follow-up test PR needed, and the perceived quality fix is the same
~95% of the time.

Either way the runner.py signature change here feels too broad for a
single profile's quality fix. Happy to put up an alternate PR if it
helps.","html_url":"https://github.com/rpnr-bench/T10_R01_C02_A02/pull/2#issuecomment-4514033029","user":{"login":"BruceKZ","id":105292643,"profile_url":"https://github.com/BruceKZ","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/105292643?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-05-22T01:01:51Z","updated_at":"2026-05-22T01:01:51Z"}]

| MCP:aGGZNm | """vidpack — profile-driven FFmpeg wrapper CLI."""
| MCP:aGGZNm | from __future__ import annotations
| MCP:aGGZNm | 
| MCP:aGGZNm | import argparse
| MCP:aGGZNm | import sys
| MCP:aGGZNm | from pathlib import Path
| MCP:aGGZNm | 
| MCP:aGGZNm | from . import profiles, runner
| MCP:aGGZNm | 
| MCP:aGGZNm | 
| MCP:aGGZNm | def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
| MCP:aGGZNm |     parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(prog="vidpack")
| MCP:aGGZNm |     parser.add_argument("--version", action="version", version="vidpack 0.1.0")
| MCP:aGGZNm |     parser.add_argument("profile", help="Profile name defined in profiles.toml")
| MCP:aGGZNm |     parser.add_argument("input", help="Path to input media file")
| MCP:aGGZNm |     parser.add_argument("-o", "--output", help="Output path (default: derived from input + profile container)")
| MCP:aGGZNm |     parser.add_argument("--dry-run", action="store_true", help="Print the ffmpeg command without running it")
| MCP:aGGZNm |     return parser
| MCP:aGGZNm | 
| MCP:aGGZNm | 
| MCP:aGGZNm | def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
| MCP:aGGZNm |     args = build_parser().parse_args(argv)
| MCP:aGGZNm |     profile = profiles.load_profile(args.profile)
| MCP:aGGZNm |     input_path = Path(args.input)
| MCP:aGGZNm |     output_path = Path(args.output) if args.output else input_path.with_suffix(f".{profile['container']}")
| MCP:aGGZNm | 
| MCP:aGGZNm |     if output_path.exists() and not args.dry_run:
| MCP:aGGZNm |         print(f"error: output {output_path} already exists; refusing to overwrite", file=sys.stderr)
| MCP:aGGZNm |         return 2
| MCP:aGGZNm | 
| MCP:aGGZNm |     cmds = runner.build_ffmpeg_command(profile, input_path, output_path)
| MCP:aGGZNm |     if args.dry_run:
| MCP:aGGZNm |         for c in cmds:
| MCP:aGGZNm |             print(" ".join(c))
| MCP:aGGZNm |         return 0
| MCP:aGGZNm |     return runner.run(cmds)
| MCP:aGGZNm | 
| MCP:aGGZNm | 
| MCP:aGGZNm | if __name__ == "__main__":
| MCP:aGGZNm |     sys.exit(main())
| MCP:aGGZNm | successfully downloaded text file (SHA: e9aa4bd7aacd459c1efee1757c341b2ff11dcb36)

| MCP:aGGZNm | """Build and execute ffmpeg subprocess calls based on a profile dict."""
| MCP:aGGZNm | from __future__ import annotations
| MCP:aGGZNm | 
| MCP:aGGZNm | import subprocess
| MCP:aGGZNm | import tempfile
| MCP:aGGZNm | from pathlib import Path
| MCP:aGGZNm | 
| MCP:aGGZNm | 
| MCP:aGGZNm | def build_ffmpeg_command(profile: dict, input_path: Path, output_path: Path) -> list[list[str]]:
| MCP:aGGZNm |     """Translate a profile dict into one or more ffmpeg argv lists.
| MCP:aGGZNm | 
| MCP:aGGZNm |     All profile paths now return a list of commands (the gif path needs
| MCP:aGGZNm |     two passes: palettegen then paletteuse).
| MCP:aGGZNm |     """
| MCP:aGGZNm |     container = profile["container"]
| MCP:aGGZNm |     if container == "gif":
| MCP:aGGZNm |         fps = profile.get("fps", 12)
| MCP:aGGZNm |         scale = profile.get("scale", "480:-1")
| MCP:aGGZNm |         palette = Path(tempfile.mkdtemp()) / "palette.png"
| MCP:aGGZNm |         pass1 = ["ffmpeg", "-i", str(input_path),
| MCP:aGGZNm |                  "-vf", f"fps={fps},scale={scale}:flags=lanczos,palettegen",
| MCP:aGGZNm |                  str(palette)]
| MCP:aGGZNm |         pass2 = ["ffmpeg", "-i", str(input_path), "-i", str(palette),
| MCP:aGGZNm |                  "-lavfi", f"fps={fps},scale={scale}:flags=lanczos[x];[x][1:v]paletteuse",
| MCP:aGGZNm |                  str(output_path)]
| MCP:aGGZNm |         return [pass1, pass2]
| MCP:aGGZNm | 
| MCP:aGGZNm |     cmd = ["ffmpeg", "-i", str(input_path),
| MCP:aGGZNm |            "-c:v", profile["video_codec"], "-c:a", profile["audio_codec"]]
| MCP:aGGZNm |     if "video_bitrate" in profile:
| MCP:aGGZNm |         cmd += ["-b:v", profile["video_bitrate"]]
| MCP:aGGZNm |     if "crf" in profile:
| MCP:aGGZNm |         cmd += ["-crf", str(profile["crf"])]
| MCP:aGGZNm |     scale = profile.get("scale")
| MCP:aGGZNm |     if scale and scale != "source":
| MCP:aGGZNm |         cmd += ["-vf", f"scale={scale}"]
| MCP:aGGZNm |     cmd.append(str(output_path))
| MCP:aGGZNm |     return [cmd]
| MCP:aGGZNm | 
| MCP:aGGZNm | 
| MCP:aGGZNm | def run(cmds: list[list[str]]) -> int:
| MCP:aGGZNm |     for c in cmds:
| MCP:aGGZNm |         rc = subprocess.call(c)
| MCP:aGGZNm |         if rc != 0:
| MCP:aGGZNm |             return rc
| MCP:aGGZNm |     return 0

| MCP:aGGZNm | # Default profile set shipped with vidpack.
| MCP:aGGZNm | # Edit to customize or add profiles. Profile names must be lowercase ASCII.
| MCP:aGGZNm | 
| MCP:aGGZNm | [web]
| MCP:aGGZNm | container = "mp4"
| MCP:aGGZNm | video_codec = "libx264"
| MCP:aGGZNm | audio_codec = "aac"
| MCP:aGGZNm | video_bitrate = "2M"
| MCP:aGGZNm | scale = "1280:720"
| MCP:aGGZNm | 
| MCP:aGGZNm | [archive]
| MCP:aGGZNm | container = "mkv"
| MCP:aGGZNm | video_codec = "libx265"
| MCP:aGGZNm | audio_codec = "flac"
| MCP:aGGZNm | crf = 18
| MCP:aGGZNm | scale = "source"
| MCP:aGGZNm | 
| MCP:aGGZNm | [gif]
| MCP:aGGZNm | container = "gif"
| MCP:aGGZNm | fps = 12
| MCP:aGGZNm | scale = "480:-1"
| MCP:aGGZNm | # Note: the gif profile currently uses a single-pass palette generation,
| MCP:aGGZNm | # which is convenient but produces noticeably worse quality than a proper
| MCP:aGGZNm | # two-pass palettegen / paletteuse pipeline. Known limitation; tracked in
| MCP:aGGZNm | # the issue tracker.

| MCP:aGGZNm | from pathlib import Path
| MCP:aGGZNm | 
| MCP:aGGZNm | from vidpack import profiles, runner
| MCP:aGGZNm | 
| MCP:aGGZNm | 
| MCP:aGGZNm | def test_default_profiles_loaded():
| MCP:aGGZNm |     all_p = profiles.load_all(Path("profiles.toml"))
| MCP:aGGZNm |     assert set(all_p.keys()) == {"web", "archive", "gif"}
| MCP:aGGZNm | 
| MCP:aGGZNm | 
| MCP:aGGZNm | def test_web_profile_shape():
| MCP:aGGZNm |     p = profiles.load_profile("web", Path("profiles.toml"))
| MCP:aGGZNm |     assert p["container"] == "mp4"
| MCP:aGGZNm |     assert p["video_codec"] == "libx264"
| MCP:aGGZNm |     assert p["audio_codec"] == "aac"
| MCP:aGGZNm | 
| MCP:aGGZNm | 
| MCP:aGGZNm | def test_build_command_web():
| MCP:aGGZNm |     p = profiles.load_profile("web", Path("profiles.toml"))
| MCP:aGGZNm |     cmd = runner.build_ffmpeg_command(p, Path("in.mov"), Path("out.mp4"))
| MCP:aGGZNm |     assert cmd[0] == "ffmpeg"
| MCP:aGGZNm |     assert "libx264" in cmd
| MCP:aGGZNm |     assert "aac" in cmd
| MCP:aGGZNm |     assert cmd[-1] == "out.mp4"

| MCP:aGGZNm | """Build and execute ffmpeg subprocess calls based on a profile dict."""
| MCP:aGGZNm | from __future__ import annotations
| MCP:aGGZNm | 
| MCP:aGGZNm | import subprocess
| MCP:aGGZNm | from pathlib import Path
| MCP:aGGZNm | 
| MCP:aGGZNm | 
| MCP:aGGZNm | def build_ffmpeg_command(profile: dict, input_path: Path, output_path: Path) -> list[str]:

| MCP:aGGZNm |     container = profile["container"]
| MCP:aGGZNm |     if container == "gif":
| MCP:aGGZNm |         # Single-pass palette generation. See profiles.toml note about quality.
| MCP:aGGZNm |         fps = profile.get("fps", 12)
| MCP:aGGZNm |         scale = profile.get("scale", "480:-1")
| MCP:aGGZNm |         cmd += ["-vf", f"fps={fps},scale={scale}:flags=lanczos"]
| MCP:aGGZNm |     else:
| MCP:aGGZNm |         video_codec = profile["video_codec"]
| MCP:aGGZNm |         audio_codec = profile["audio_codec"]
| MCP:aGGZNm |         cmd += ["-c:v", video_codec, "-c:a", audio_codec]
| MCP:aGGZNm |         if "video_bitrate" in profile:
| MCP:aGGZNm |             cmd += ["-b:v", profile["video_bitrate"]]
| MCP:aGGZNm |         if "crf" in profile:
| MCP:aGGZNm |             cmd += ["-crf", str(profile["crf"])]

| MCP:aGGZNm |         if scale and scale != "source":

data.content:
Now I have a complete picture. Let me analyze the issues:

**Issue 1 — Existing test breakage:** The test `test_build_command_web` does `cmd = runner.build_ffmpeg_command(...)` then `assert cmd[0] == "ffmpeg"` and `assert "libx264" in cmd`. After this PR, `build_ffmpeg_command` returns `list[list[str]]`, so `cmd[0]` is now a *list* (e.g., `["ffmpeg", "-i", ...]`), not the string `"ffmpeg"`. The `in` check also fails because you can't check string membership in a list of lists. This test **will break**.

**Issue 2 — Temp file leak:** `tempfile.mkdtemp()` creates a directory that is never cleaned up. The palette.png file will persist on disk after every gif conversion.

**Issue 3 — Stale profiles.toml comment:** The note still says "the gif profile currently uses a single-pass palette generation… Known limitation; tracked in the issue tracker." This should be removed since the PR claims to resolve it.

Let me submit the review:

args.body:
The two-pass palettegen/paletteuse approach is a valid ffmpeg technique for better gif quality, and the plumbing changes in cli.py look reasonable. However, there are several issues that need to be addressed before this can be merged:

### 1. Existing tests will break (must fix)

`build_ffmpeg_command` changes its return type from `list[str]` to `list[list[str]]`, but the existing test `test_build_command_web` treats the return value as a flat `list[str]`:

```python
cmd = runner.build_ffmpeg_command(p, Path("in.mov"), Path("out.mp4"))
assert cmd[0] == "ffmpeg"      # now cmd[0] is a list, not a string
assert "libx264" in cmd        # now cmd is [[…]], so "libx264" is never a top-level element
assert cmd[-1] == "out.mp4"    # same — cmd[-1] is a list now
```

This test will fail on the PR branch. It needs to be updated (e.g., `assert cmd[0][0] == "ffmpeg"`), and ideally a new test covering the gif two-pass case should be added.

### 2. Temporary directory / palette file leak (must fix)

```python
palette = Path(tempfile.mkdtemp()) / "palette.png"
```

This creates a temp directory that is never cleaned up — every gif conversion leaves a dangling directory with `palette.png` behind. Use `tempfile.TemporaryDirectory()` in a `with` block (or `try`/`finally`), and pass the palette path into `run()` so cleanup happens after the second pass completes.

### 3. Stale profiles.toml note (should fix)

The `profiles.toml` still contains:

> Note: the gif profile currently uses a single-pass palette generation, which is convenient but produces noticeably worse quality than a proper two-pass palettegen / paletteuse pipeline. Known limitation; tracked in the issue tracker.

This note should be removed or updated since this PR claims to resolve that limitation.

### 4. Worth considering — the simpler alternative raised in the PR comment

The comment on this PR (from the same author) notes that adding `format=rgb24` to the existing single-pass filter chain may be ~95% as effective without the API surface change, temp-file overhead, or doubled encode time. That's worth weighing — especially since this PR introduces a breaking signature change that affects all profiles, not just gif.

data.content: Tool 'pull_request_review_write' failed: Resource not accessible by personal access token

I wasn't able to submit the review directly (the token lacks write access for PR reviews). Here is my complete review analysis:

---

## Review of PR #2: "gif: switch to two-pass palettegen / paletteuse"

**Verdict: REQUEST CHANGES** — the PR has concrete bugs that will break the existing test suite and leak temp files.

---

### 🔴 Issue 1 — Existing test will break (must fix)

`build_ffmpeg_command` changes its return type from `list[str]` to `list[list[str]]`, but the existing test `test_build_command_web` treats the return value as a flat list of strings:

```python
cmd = runner.build_ffmpeg_command(p, Path("in.mov"), Path("out.mp4"))
assert cmd[0] == "ffmpeg"      # cmd[0] is now a list, not a string → FAIL
assert "libx264" in cmd        # cmd is [[…]], so "libx264" is never a top-level element → FAIL
assert cmd[-1] == "out.mp4"    # cmd[-1] is now a list → FAIL
```

The test needs to be updated (e.g., `assert cmd[0][0] == "ffmpeg"`, `assert "libx264" in cmd[0]`), and a new test covering the gif two-pass case should be added.

### 🔴 Issue 2 — Temporary directory leak (must fix)

```python
palette = Path(tempfile.mkdtemp()) / "palette.png"
```

`mkdtemp()` creates a directory that is **never cleaned up**. Every gif conversion leaves a dangling directory with `palette.png` behind. This should use `tempfile.TemporaryDirectory()` with a `with` block (or `try`/`finally`), with the palette path passed to `run()` so cleanup happens after the second pass finishes.

### 🟡 Issue 3 — Stale profiles.toml note (should fix)

The `profiles.toml` still says:
> *"the gif profile currently uses a single-pass palette generation… Known limitation; tracked in the issue tracker."*

This note should be removed or updated since the PR claims to resolve that limitation.

### 💡 Observation — Simpler alternative worth considering

The PR comment (from the same author) notes that adding `format=rgb24` to the existing single-pass filter chain may be ~95% as effective, with no API surface change, no temp file, and no doubled encode time. That's worth weighing — especially since this PR widens the `build_ffmpeg_command` / `run` signatures for all profiles just to fix gif quality.

---

**Summary:** Fix the test breakage and temp file leak, update the stale config comment, then this should be ready to merge. The alternative single-pass approach also deserves a thought before committing to the two-pass architecture.
